title: Allocarya chorisiana
taxonomy: Allocarya chorisiana is classified within the plant family Boraginaceae. This family includes about 1,200 species of flowering plants commonly known as borage, forget-me-nots, and heliotropes. Allocarya chorisiana is a type of flowering plant, specifically a shrub, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its classification also includes its genus Allocarya, which belongs to the tribe Boragineae. The Boragineae tribe is part of the larger order Lamiales in the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group III classification.

intro: Allocarya chorisiana, commonly known as Chisana or Choisiana, is a perennial plant species native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Allocarya chorisiana include essential oils, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
